summary: DHH criticizes Google's plan to modify web content by automatically inserting sponsored links, such as converting ISBN references into Amazon affiliate links. He agrees with Robert Scoble of Microsoft and Dave Winer that this approach excludes content authors from the decision. DHH calls it a 'terrible idea' and 'evil doing,' urging Google to reverse course immediately.
key_points: Google proposed automatically rewriting web content to insert sponsored links like ISBN-to-Amazon conversions
The initiative excludes content authors from having a say in how their content is modified
DHH frames Google's action as a violation of its 'Don't be evil' motto
money_quotes: The choice Google's DiBona talks about excludes content authors from the discussion.
Google needs to back paddle out of this evil doing. Post haste.
key_insight: Platforms that modify publishers' content without their consent are overstepping their role, regardless of how much market power they hold.
